title: Two Sum
constraints: Return indices of two distinct elements whose values sum to target.
statement:
## Problem

Return the indices of two numbers in `nums` whose sum is `target`.

## Input / Output

- `nums`: `list[int]`, the input numbers.
- `target`: `int`, the desired sum.
- Return: `list[int]`, two indices in increasing order.
theory: A hash map stores previously seen values so each new value can look up its complement in constant average time.

examples.name: Walkthrough
examples.body: For `nums = [2,7,11,15]` and `target = 9`, when we see $7$, its complement $2$ is already stored, so return `[0,1]`.

starter_code:
def two_sum(nums: list[int], target: int) -> list[int]:
    pass
solution_explanation: Pseudocode: scan values, compute the complement, return the stored index when present, otherwise store the current value. In engineering terms, this avoids a nested loop and keeps memory proportional to the number of values scanned.
